title: TA505 / Clop Ransomware Adversary Emulation
subtitle: Threat-informed emulation of a financially-motivated big-game intrusion against a flat, single-domain Windows estate (~1,600 servers, ~7,300 workstations). Range / tabletop exercise, derived from the publicly documented 2019 university incident.

narrative_paras: This plan emulates TA505 (a.k.a. Grace / Gold Tahoe), a patient, hands-on-keyboard e-crime group whose objective is an enterprise-wide Clop ransomware detonation for ransom. It reproduces the documented 2019 chain: a phished macro-Excel foothold, quiet C2 beaconing, exploitation of unpatched Windows servers, AD recon to Domain Admin, Cobalt Strike on the domain controllers, AV disablement, then mass encryption.

The point of the exercise is the detection scorecard, not the encryption. On a flat domain with no central SIEM, the emulation predicts that most pre-ransomware steps generate telemetry that is collected-but-unwatched or not collected at all, meaning the ~5-week window during which the attacker held full domain admin would most likely pass unseen.
systemic_issues: No central log aggregation or 24/7 monitoring: telemetry exists on islands nobody reads.
Flat, unsegmented single AD domain: one foothold reaches Domain Admin with commodity tooling.
Unpatched internet-reachable Windows servers (MS17-010 ~2 years unpatched) and online-reachable backups.
exec.ask_of_management: Fund central log collection + MDR and network segmentation before the next patch cycle; both defeat the highest-leverage steps in this chain.

in_scope.asset: Isolated range mirroring the flat AD domain
Emulated mail/Exchange, file shares, domain controllers

out_of_scope.asset: Any production tenant or live user data

scope.deconfliction: White cell on Signal; 15-min SLA; safety lead holds abort
stop_conditions: Any real customer or personal data touched
Any unplanned service outage beyond the range
exclusions: No real ransomware encryption; a benign marker file stands in for sage.exe / swaqp.exe
No data exfiltration of real PII; exfil is simulated to a controlled sink

adversary.name: TA505 (Grace / SectorJ04 / Gold Tahoe)
adversary.sector_geo_fit: Opportunistic big-game against large, flat estates; education/public-sector precedent
adversary.source_intrusion: Publicly documented 2019 university-wide Clop deployment (267 Windows servers encrypted; ~69-day dwell; full DA reached ~5 weeks before detonation).
objectives: Reach and hold Domain Admin quietly
Stage a control layer on the domain controllers
Disable endpoint AV, then detonate enterprise-wide
flags: Cobalt Strike beacon on a DC
Encryption marker on the backup plane

runners_up.name: Generic ransomware affiliate (e.g. LockBit-style)
runners_up.reason: Less faithful to the documented incident's dual-use, low-and-slow tradecraft

runners_up.name: Nation-state APT
runners_up.reason: Wrong motivation and over-scoped for an SME-style estate

sources.title: Publicly documented 2019 incident report and IoCs

killchain.procedure_detail: Two lure emails ('Documents', 'CL meeting schedule.xls'); user opens link, downloads macro-Excel
killchain.expected_observable: Proxy/DNS to windows-en-us-update.com; mail gateway link
Office spawning script host; child process tree

killchain.procedure_detail: SDBBot beacons ~every 15 min; Meterpreter added for interactive access
EternalBlue / MS17-010 against unpatched Windows servers
killchain.expected_observable: SMBv1 exploit signatures; anomalous service creation

killchain.procedure_detail: PowerSploit, PingCastle, AdFind map AD and privileged accounts
Mimikatz dumps credentials; reaches full Domain Admin
Cobalt Strike planted on domain controllers as the control layer
Using stolen admin, disable McAfee / Defender fleet-wide before firing
killchain.expected_observable: Mass AV-disable / service-stop events across hosts

killchain.procedure_detail: Deploy Clop (marker file) to domain-joined servers incl. reachable backups; T1490 inhibit recovery
killchain.expected_observable: Mass file-rename; encryption marker on backup plane

scorecard.analyst_note: Logs exist on-box; nobody reviews; no alerting
If EDR present with tamper protection, Office-spawns-script fires
14-day firewall retention too short for periodicity hunt
No central IDS; server event logs unaggregated
Only if Sysmon deployed to Tier-0; partial coverage
DCs often lack EDR in SME estates
Fleet-wide disable is loud IF anything watches it
Detected only when files are already encrypting

scorecard_summary.mean_time_to_detect: not detected pre-impact in the baseline estate

findings.title: Flat single AD domain lets one foothold reach Domain Admin

exploitability.rationale: Commodity tooling (Mimikatz, PowerSploit) on an unsegmented domain

blast_radius.rationale: Domain Admin = whole estate incl. backups

findings.description: No tiering or segmentation between workstations, servers, and domain controllers, so lateral movement and privilege escalation face no internal boundary.
findings.remediation: Introduce AD tiering (Tier-0 isolation), segment the server and backup planes, and remove standing Domain Admin.
findings.detection_fix: Deploy Sysmon + EDR to Tier-0; alert on LSASS access and DC logon anomalies.

findings.title: MS17-010 unpatched on internet-reachable Windows servers

blast_radius.rationale: Direct server compromise and lateral spread

findings.description: A patch available since 2017 was not applied, giving a reliable remote-code-execution path into the server estate.
findings.remediation: Emergency patch MS17-010; set a patch SLA for internet-reachable and Tier-0 systems.
findings.detection_fix: IDS signature for EternalBlue; alert on anomalous SMBv1 and new service creation.

findings.title: No central log collection or monitoring of pre-ransomware telemetry

exploitability.rationale: Not directly exploitable, but removes every detection opportunity

blast_radius.rationale: The entire dwell window passes unseen

findings.description: Telemetry that would catch beaconing, AD recon, and C2 on DCs exists only as unaggregated island logs with short retention and no reviewer.
findings.remediation: Stand up MDR or central log collection for EDR, DC auth, DNS, and proxy with adequate retention.
findings.detection_fix: Beacon-periodicity analytic on aggregated DNS/firewall logs; AD-recon analytic on DC logs.

deconfliction_events.description: Range IDS flagged exploit traffic; confirmed in-scope with white cell

safety.evidence_handling: All artefacts retained on the isolated range evidence store; destroyed after the retest window per RoE.

seats.name: Threat intelligence
seats.stance: Emulate TA505 faithfully to the documented chain
seats.summary: TA505 is the right, realistic actor: financially motivated, patient, dual-use tooling. The chain mirrors the 2019 incident so the blue team scores against a real precedent.

seats.name: Red-team operator
seats.stance: The chain is reproducible on the range with atomic tests
seats.summary: Each step has a re-runnable atomic test. The high-leverage, quietest steps (beaconing, AD recon, C2 on DCs) are exactly the ones the current estate would miss.

seats.name: Safety lead
seats.stance: Go, range-only, with marker files for impact
seats.summary: No real encryption or exfil. Impact steps are simulated. Abort authority held; deconfliction channel confirmed before start.

verified: The 2019 incident IoCs and ATT&CK mappings are from public documentation and safe for authorized emulation.
Range mirrors the flat single-domain topology under test.
unverified: Exact EDR/Sysmon coverage of the real estate (assumed thin) — confirm before reading the scorecard as the production result.
Whether firewall/DNS retention in production exceeds the 14 days assumed here.
